component.name: Modal
component.description: A dialog layered over a dimmed blanket, portalled to document.body. Interrupts the flow to demand a focused decision or task — confirming an action, capturing input, or surfacing content that must be dealt with before continuing. Composed from ModalHeader, ModalBody, and ModalFooter.
component.note: White card with SHARP corners (radius-none, DS convention) and --shadow-overlay over a one-off ink blanket rgba(5,12,31,0.46). Four exported parts: Modal / ModalHeader / ModalBody / ModalFooter, wired together via context. Traps focus, locks body scroll, restores focus to the trigger on close. Synced to Figma '❖ Modal dialog'.

blanket.gutter: spacing.3xl (32px) padding around the card; 0 for full-screen

card.maxHeight: calc(100vh - 2 * spacing.3xl); none when shouldScrollInViewport; 100vh for full-screen

size.full-screen: fills the viewport (width/height 100%, no shadow, no gutter)

header.gap: spacing.md (16px) title-row ↔ close; spacing.base (8px) icon ↔ title
header.title: Inter bold 20/24 (font-size.lg / line-height.sm, font-weight.bold), colors.text.neutral.strong (--text-neutral-strong, neutral.800)
header.leadingIcon: 20px Icon, colors.text.neutral.default; appearance=danger defaults to a 'warning' glyph tinted colors.text.danger.default (--text-danger-default, danger.500)

body.padding: 0 spacing.xl (0 24px; header/footer give the vertical room)

useCases: Confirm a consequential action before proceeding
Capture a short focused form or input without leaving the page
Surface content the user must read or act on before continuing
Present an isolated full-screen task (full-screen size)
requiredProps: ModalHeader title (or aria-label on Modal when there is no title)

commonPatterns.name: Confirmation dialog
commonPatterns.description: A small modal with a title, a sentence of body copy, and a Cancel + Confirm footer.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Modal isOpen={open} onClose={close} size='small'><ModalHeader title='Publish article?' /><ModalBody>This will make the article visible to everyone.</ModalBody><ModalFooter><Button appearance='subtle' onClick={close}>Cancel</Button><Button appearance='primary' onClick={confirm}>Publish</Button></ModalFooter></Modal>

commonPatterns.name: Destructive confirmation
commonPatterns.description: appearance=danger surfaces a warning icon in the header; the consumer pairs it with a danger Confirm button.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Modal isOpen={open} onClose={close} appearance='danger' size='small'><ModalHeader title='Delete account?' /><ModalBody>This permanently removes your account and cannot be undone.</ModalBody><ModalFooter><Button appearance='subtle' onClick={close}>Cancel</Button><Button appearance='danger' onClick={confirm}>Delete</Button></ModalFooter></Modal>

commonPatterns.name: Form modal
commonPatterns.description: A medium/large modal whose body holds form fields; the body scrolls while header and footer stay pinned.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Modal isOpen={open} onClose={close} size='large'><ModalHeader title='Edit profile' /><ModalBody><TextField label='Name' /><TextField label='Email' /></ModalBody><ModalFooter><Button appearance='subtle' onClick={close}>Cancel</Button><Button appearance='primary' onClick={save}>Save</Button></ModalFooter></Modal>

commonPatterns.name: Custom leading icon
commonPatterns.description: Pass an explicit icon to ModalHeader to override the default (or to add one to a default-appearance modal).

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a modal for a non-blocking notification or status message
antiPatterns.reason: Modals seize focus and block the page; a transient or low-priority message should not interrupt the user.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use Banner for persistent in-page messages or Toast for transient confirmations.

antiPatterns.scenario: Nesting a modal inside another open modal
antiPatterns.reason: Stacked dialogs break the focus trap and disorient users; only one blocking surface should be active.
antiPatterns.alternative: Replace the content of the existing modal, or use a multi-step flow within one modal.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using full-screen size for a short confirmation
antiPatterns.reason: Full-screen overwhelms a one-line decision and removes the surrounding context unnecessarily.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use size='small' (or 'medium') for confirmations; reserve full-screen for immersive tasks.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using appearance=danger for a non-destructive action
antiPatterns.reason: The warning icon and danger Confirm signal irreversible consequences; misusing them erodes the cue.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use appearance='default' for ordinary confirmations; reserve danger for destructive/irreversible actions.

antiPatterns.scenario: Rendering a Modal with neither a ModalHeader title nor an aria-label
antiPatterns.reason: The dialog has no accessible name, so screen readers announce it without context.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always include a ModalHeader title, or set aria-label on Modal when there is no visible title.

slots.children: The dialog contents — typically ModalHeader, ModalBody, and ModalFooter in that order
slots.ModalHeader.title: Heading text; also sets the dialog's accessible name
slots.ModalHeader.icon: Optional leading icon (defaults to 'warning' when appearance=danger)

parentConstraints: Portalled to document.body — renders outside the DOM position where it is declared, independent of any parent layout or overflow

interactions.open: Focus moves into the dialog (first focusable element, or the dialog itself); body scroll is locked
interactions.focusTrap: Tab and Shift+Tab cycle within the dialog and never leave it
interactions.close: Escape, blanket click, or the header close button calls onClose; focus is restored to the previously-focused trigger
interactions.shouldCloseOnEscape: Escape closes the modal; defaults to true, set false to require an explicit action
interactions.shouldCloseOnBlanketClick: Clicking the dimmed blanket (not the card) closes the modal; defaults to true
interactions.shouldScrollInViewport: Switches from body-only scroll to whole-overlay scroll (body overflow becomes visible, overlay aligns to top); defaults to false
interactions.bodyScroll: By default the ModalBody scrolls while header and footer stay pinned

behavior.controlled: Always controlled: render based on isOpen and update it from onClose. The component returns null when isOpen is false.

accessibility.ariaModal: aria-modal='true' marks content outside the dialog inert to assistive tech
accessibility.accessibleName: aria-labelledby points at the ModalHeader title; falls back to aria-label on Modal when no title is present
accessibility.accessibleDescription: ModalBody carries an id wired through context for description association
accessibility.focusManagement: Focus moves into the dialog on open and is restored to the trigger on close; a manual focus trap keeps Tab cycling within the dialog (dialog itself has tabIndex=-1)
accessibility.scrollRegion: ModalBody has tabIndex=0 so the scrollable region is keyboard-reachable (satisfies axe scrollable-region-focusable)
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Tab / Shift+Tab cycle focus within the dialog; Escape closes (when shouldCloseOnEscape); Enter/Space activate the focused button
accessibility.screenReader: Announced as a dialog with its title as the name; background scroll is locked and outside content is inert
accessibility.contrastNote: neutral.800 title and body text on white, the danger.500 header icon, and the forest.700 focus ring all meet AA.

athenaConstraints.dangerCue: appearance=danger tints the header icon danger.500; the danger Confirm button is the consumer's choice, not automatic

figmaDivergences.reason: Athena DS convention is sharp corners across all surfaces.

figmaDivergences.figma: danger appearance implies a danger.500-tinted Confirm
figmaDivergences.implementation: the Confirm uses the Button component, which keeps the danger fill at danger.700 for AA contrast
figmaDivergences.reason: Button owns its own fills; danger.700 meets AA where danger.500 would not.
Accessibility addition so the scrollable region is keyboard-reachable (axe scrollable-region-focusable).

aiHints.context: Reach for Modal when the user must make a focused decision or complete a task before continuing — confirmations, short forms, or content that must be dealt with. Compose ModalHeader + ModalBody + ModalFooter, drive visibility with isOpen, and use appearance='danger' only for destructive/irreversible actions (pair it with a danger Confirm Button). Use small for confirmations and full-screen only for immersive tasks.
neverUseWhen: The message is non-blocking or transient — use Banner or Toast
A modal is already open — never nest modals
The action is a short confirmation — do not use full-screen size
The action is not destructive — do not use appearance='danger'
There is no ModalHeader title and no aria-label — the dialog has no accessible name
